examples.title: System Call Example - Process Creation
examples.description: Demonstrates how system calls fork() and exec() are used to create and run new processes in C
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates three important system calls: fork() creates a new process by duplicating the parent, getpid() returns process ID, execl() loads a new program into the current process. The fork() returns twice - once in parent and once in child.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for fork, O(n) for exec where n is program size
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is process memory image

examples.title: System Call Simulation - File Operations
examples.description: Shows how Python uses OS system calls for file operations
examples.code:
import os
import sys

# Using system calls through Python wrapper

# System call: open() - opens/creates file
fd = os.open("sample.txt", os.O_CREAT | os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)

# System call: write() - writes data to file
os.write(fd, b"Hello, Operating System!")

# System call: close() - closes file descriptor
os.close(fd)

# System call: open() again to read
fd = os.open("sample.txt", os.O_RDONLY)

# System call: read() - reads data from file
data = os.read(fd, 100)
print("Read from file:", data.decode())

# System call: close()
os.close(fd)

# System call: unlink() - deletes file
os.unlink("sample.txt")
print("File deleted successfully")
examples.explanation: Python wraps OS system calls (open, read, write, close, unlink) to provide file operations. Each call requests kernel services for device access. The file descriptor is a small integer returned by open() representing an entry in the system file table.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for read/write where n is bytes transferred

examples.title: Process Creation Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm demonstrating how the OS creates a new process using system calls
examples.explanation: The CreateProcess algorithm shows how OS uses system calls internally to create a new process. It allocates a Process Control Block (PCB) which stores all process information, allocates memory, loads the program code, initializes CPU registers and state, and adds the process to the ready queue for execution.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is memory size to allocate

examples.title: Process Management via Runtime
examples.description: Java program demonstrating OS process creation using Runtime class
examples.explanation: Java's Runtime.exec() creates OS processes through system calls. The program demonstrates starting external processes (like notepad), managing their lifecycle (isAlive, destroy), handling I/O streams, and waiting for process completion. Each operation ultimately makes system calls to the kernel.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for starting process, O(n) for I/O operations
